title: Add SecurityContext to all Job and CronJob pod specs
summary: Adds a shared restrictedSecurityContext() helper in reconcile_deployment.go that returns a container-level SecurityContext satisfying the Pod Security Standards Restricted profile (AllowPrivilegeEscalation=false, RunAsNonRoot=true, ReadOnlyRootFilesystem=true, SeccompProfile=RuntimeDefault). The helper is applied to all 6 containers across 4 builder functions: buildDBSyncJob (1 container), buildBootstrapJob (1 container), fernetRotationCronJob (2 containers), and credentialRotationCronJob (2 containers). Five new unit tests verify the SecurityContext on all containers. All existing tests pass without modification.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist for db-sync Job SecurityContext (TestBuildDBSyncJob_SecurityContext)
Tests exist for bootstrap Job SecurityContext (TestBuildBootstrapJob_SecurityContext)
Tests exist for fernet CronJob SecurityContext on both containers (TestFernetRotationCronJob_SecurityContext)
Tests exist for credential CronJob SecurityContext on both containers (TestCredentialRotationCronJob_SecurityContext)
All existing controller tests pass without modification
All 4 SecurityContext fields verified per container

code_quality_checklist.item: Shared helper prevents drift across builders
Helper placed alongside existing helpers in reconcile_deployment.go
Uses ptr.To() consistent with existing codebase usage
No code duplication in production code
Godoc comment includes CC-0045 feature ID

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal — one helper, one call per container
No API type changes, no CRD regeneration needed

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated production code — single helper used everywhere
No over-engineering — returns static struct, no configuration
No future-proofing — exactly what was requested

fail_fast_checklist.item: N/A — pure constructor function with no error paths

defensive_checklist.item: N/A — no external inputs to validate

issues_found.description: TestBuildDBSyncJob_SecurityContext accesses Containers[0] without a prior NotTo(BeEmpty()) guard, while the sibling TestBuildBootstrapJob_SecurityContext does include this guard. On an empty slice, the test would panic with an index-out-of-range instead of producing a clear gomega failure message.
issues_found.fix: Add g.Expect(job.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers).NotTo(BeEmpty()) before accessing Containers[0], matching the bootstrap test's pattern.
issues_found.evidence: container := job.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[0]  // no guard, cf. bootstrap test line 318: g.Expect(job.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers).NotTo(BeEmpty())

suggested_improvements: Consider applying restrictedSecurityContext() to the Deployment's keystone-api container as well (separate ticket — the Deployment currently has no container-level SecurityContext, which means it relies solely on the PodSecurityPolicy/PodSecurityStandards admission controller for enforcement rather than being explicitly hardened).

detected_patterns.name: Shared restrictedSecurityContext helper for PSS Restricted compliance
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a new Job or CronJob builder function that creates pod specs for the Keystone operator (or future operators following the same pattern)
detected_patterns.description: All Job and CronJob container specs must include SecurityContext: restrictedSecurityContext() on every container (including init containers). The restrictedSecurityContext() helper in reconcile_deployment.go returns a *corev1.SecurityContext with AllowPrivilegeEscalation=ptr.To(false), RunAsNonRoot=ptr.To(true), ReadOnlyRootFilesystem=ptr.To(true), and SeccompProfile={Type: RuntimeDefault}. This ensures Pod Security Standards Restricted profile compliance. The helper is used by 4 builder functions across 6 containers.
